strengths: Extensive backend and distributed systems experience, including building micro-services infrastructure handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby.
Demonstrated architectural initiative by founding and developing an event-driven, open-source Geo-Data platform (GISCollective) deployed on Kubernetes.
Recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, including Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators at re:cinq.
concerns: Completely lacks executive-level engineering leadership experience; has not scaled teams from 10 to 30+ people or developed senior technical leadership layers.
Missing extensive production experience designing and operating complex, multi-agent AI systems in regulated, high-stakes commercial environments.
Lacks required domain expertise in fintech infrastructure, specifically algorithmic working-capital management, dynamic exposure modeling, and real-time credit-risk assessment.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced individual contributor with strong backend and distributed systems skills, but he falls significantly short of the executive and domain requirements for this CTO role. He lacks the mandated five years of senior technical leadership, having never scaled an engineering organization, managed external delivery partners, or developed a leadership layer. Furthermore, while he has brief, recent exposure to AI tooling, he lacks the deep, production-grade experience architecting stateful multi-agent AI frameworks for high-stakes, regulated fintech environments like real-time credit risk and algorithmic working-capital management. His profile aligns with a Staff Software Engineer rather than an executive engineering leader.
